category.description: Designed linguistic category used to diversify utterances.
category.purpose: Controls phonetic and acoustic diversity across the benchmark.

General.description: Balanced everyday speech representative of normal TTS usage.

expected_characteristics: Suitable for cropping and synchronization attacks

Vowel-rich.description: Sentences intentionally containing many vowels and sonorant sounds.

Fricative-rich.description: Sentences emphasizing fricative consonants such as /s/, /f/, /ʃ/, and /z/.

Plosive-rich.description: Sentences emphasizing plosive consonants such as /p/, /t/, /k/, /b/, /d/, and /g/.

Numbers.description: Sentences containing dates, measurements, currencies, or numerical values.

Proper nouns.description: Sentences containing names of people, places, organizations, or products.
expected_characteristics: Tests pronunciation of uncommon lexical items
May induce pronunciation variability across models

Emotional.description: Sentences intended to elicit expressive speech.

duration.description: Approximate expected duration of the synthesized utterance.
duration.purpose: Used for grouping utterances by temporal length.

dominant_phonetic_class.description: Primary phonetic characteristic intentionally emphasized by the prompt.
dominant_phonetic_class.purpose: Provides a coarse phonetic grouping for later analysis.

Vowels.description: Predominantly vowel and sonorant phonemes producing smoother harmonic speech.

Fricatives.description: Predominantly fricative consonants producing broadband spectral energy.

Plosives.description: Predominantly stop consonants producing transient bursts.

Mixed.description: Combination of multiple phonetic classes without one being intentionally dominant.
